behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model diarization quality (speaker-label preservation), which a stubbed transcript would hide. This is not just transport/routing around the API; correctness is in model output content. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model boundary (speech-to-text output), not a required multi-agentic sequence assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the production speech-to-text API and assert the returned transcript preserves distinct speaker labels mapped to utterances needed for downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser/mapping logic on transcript text to a structured object; it can fail within a local function/object boundary without real infra or model dependence. Rejected Integration: wording focuses on parsing/extraction logic itself, not cross-module state propagation that only appears when modules are wired.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript strings (including edge formats for assignee/task phrasing) into the parsing function and assert exact structured fields for assignee and task text.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output quality/completeness (coverage of assignees in generated action items). Deterministic fixtures would mask omission failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior text does not require validating the full multi-stage pipeline, only this output-quality property at the summary generation boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Using transcripts with known task assignments per attendee, call the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears in at least one action item in the output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Given already-identified decisions, this is deterministic routing/mapping logic to schema fields and can be validated inside one code boundary. Rejected Integration: behavior text says "identified decisions" and "routes...into schema," which disqualifies needing real infra/model or multi-module runtime interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Call the routing function with inputs that mark items as decisions and assert they are emitted only under the `decisions` schema path, not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model judgment about extraction/placement of decision content from transcript to structured summary; stubs would always pass and miss real misclassification/omission. Rejected Workflow: behavior text focuses on one output criterion of the live summary, not an explicit multi-agentic chain requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Send transcripts containing explicit decisions to the real summarization model and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing (segment -> process each -> merge) and final summary coherence, which depends on multiple agentic outputs composed over steps. Lower levels miss cross-step degradation and merge failures that appear only across the chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through the production pipeline path that segments audio, processes segments in order via real model calls, and merges outputs; assert ordered processing occurred and final merged summary remains coherent and structurally valid.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end, real-infra, multi-stage pipeline with at least two real model-dependent stages (transcription and summarization). Real failures emerge from chained handoffs and stage interactions, so single-step Agentic/System tests are insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on a real meeting audio file from upload through final structured delivery, with no manual fixes; assert successful completion and schema-valid output containing expected top-level sections.
